summary: Read-only analysis completed for <machine-local-path> (1).md. Verified expected SHA-256, read the full 1,285-line document, and extracted durable rules around evidence lineage, provenance, source/chunk/claim separation, full-memory audit reproducibility, and proof vs non-proof boundaries.
decisions: Treat the provided local document hash match as proof of the exact document version analyzed, but not proof of the live SecondBrain state or referenced artifacts.
Treat raw source existence, external URL anchors, counts-only audits, stale snapshots, fallback/bootstrap text, and JSON/API-only UI evidence as non-proof under the document's rules.
Emphasize OS-native manifest reconciliation, content-bearing evidence chains, exact direct-MCP discovery, prompt-bound task lifecycle records, and Korean browser/DOM proof as durable proof surfaces.
next_steps: If implementation follows, start with P0-02/P0-04/P0-03/P0-05/P0-05C/P0-05B/P0-06/P0-01 in the document's stated order.
Before any final-readiness claim, run live OS-native full-memory audit reconciliation and source-lineage coverage checks against current SecondBrain rather than relying on this static plan.
Keep validation votes tied to this document SHA and require each validator's own full-memory read stats, document SHA, and evidence checked.
